title: Qualys API Rate Limits, Concurrency, Pagination, and Truncation Reference

confirmed_facts.fact: Concurrency limit: 2 simultaneous API calls per subscription (classic VM/PC APIs). Exceeding returns HTTP 409 Conflict.
Rate limit: varies by API — typically 300 calls per 24-hour rolling window for data-heavy endpoints (host detection, scan launch). Some read-only endpoints have higher limits.
Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit (total allowed), X-RateLimit-Window-Sec (window in seconds), X-RateLimit-Remaining (calls left), X-RateLimit-ToWait-Sec (seconds until reset when limited)
When rate limited: HTTP 409 with header X-RateLimit-ToWait-Sec indicating how long to wait. NOT 429 like most APIs.

Truncation (classic VM APIs): when results exceed the server-side limit, the XML response includes WARNING/TEXT containing 'Truncated' and WARNING/URL with the next-page URL containing id_min parameter.
id_min pagination: set id_min to the last host ID + 1 from the current page. The server orders results by host ID ascending. Keep fetching until no WARNING/Truncated appears.
truncation_limit parameter: set truncation_limit=0 to disable truncation on some endpoints (host list). Does NOT work on host detection list — detection list always truncates at server limit.
GAV v2 pagination: uses lastSeenAssetId cursor + hasMore boolean in JSON response. pageSize max 10000.
WAS API pagination: uses limit and offset parameters. Default limit=10, max varies by endpoint.
CSV mode truncation: the response ends with a line containing 'WARNING' and a URL with id_min — different format from XML truncation
Concurrent request to the SAME endpoint while one is running: Qualys queues it or returns 409. Concurrent requests to DIFFERENT endpoints count towards the 2-call limit.

implementation_notes:
Rate-limit-aware request wrapper:
```python
import time

def qualys_post(session, url, data, max_retries=3, timeout=(10, 300)):
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        resp = session.post(url, data=data, timeout=timeout)
        
        if resp.status_code == 409:
            wait = int(resp.headers.get('X-RateLimit-ToWait-Sec', 60))
            print(f'Rate limited, waiting {wait}s (attempt {attempt + 1})')
            time.sleep(wait)
            continue
        
        resp.raise_for_status()
        return resp
    
    raise RuntimeError(f'Qualys API rate limited after {max_retries} retries')
```

Full id_min pagination loop:
```python
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re

def paginate_qualys_xml(session, url, params: dict) -> list:
    all_elements = []
    current_params = dict(params)
    
    while True:
        resp = qualys_post(session, url, data=current_params)
        root = ET.fromstring(resp.text)
        
        # Collect data elements (adjust tag for your endpoint)
        elements = root.findall('.//HOST')
        all_elements.extend(elements)
        
        # Check truncation
        warning = root.find('.//WARNING')
        if warning is None:
            break
        text = warning.findtext('TEXT', '')
        if 'Truncated' not in text:
            break
        
        next_url = warning.findtext('URL', '')
        m = re.search(r'id_min=(\d+)', next_url)
        if not m:
            break
        current_params['id_min'] = m.group(1)
    
    return all_elements
```

Log rate limit headers for debugging:
```python
def log_rate_limits(resp):
    headers = resp.headers
    limit = headers.get('X-RateLimit-Limit', '?')
    remaining = headers.get('X-RateLimit-Remaining', '?')
    window = headers.get('X-RateLimit-Window-Sec', '?')
    print(f'Rate limit: {remaining}/{limit} remaining (window: {window}s)')
```

Pagination strategy by API family:

| API Family | Pagination Method | Key Parameters |
|---|---|---|
| Classic VM (host detection) | id_min in truncation WARNING | id_min={last_host_id + 1} |
| Classic VM (host list) | truncation_limit=0 or id_min | truncation_limit=0 disables it |
| Classic VM (KB) | id_min on QID | id_min={last_qid + 1} |
| GAV v2 | Cursor (lastSeenAssetId) | pageSize, lastSeenAssetId |
| WAS | Offset/limit | limit, offset |
| Reports | Not paginated | Single fetch per report |
caveats: Qualys uses 409 for rate limits, NOT 429 — standard retry libraries (urllib3 Retry) won't catch it unless you add 409 to status_forcelist
The 2-concurrent-call limit is per SUBSCRIPTION, not per user — parallel scripts under the same account will conflict
truncation_limit=0 does NOT work on the host detection endpoint — it always truncates. You MUST use id_min pagination.
id_min is the HOST ID (integer), not an offset or page number — it's a cursor-based approach
Long-running API calls (large detection exports) can take 5+ minutes — set read timeout to at least 300s
There is no 'next page' link in CSV mode — you must detect truncation from the response text and manually construct the next id_min request
